name: Phil Harris
abstract: Wonga Philip "Phil" Harris (June 24, 1904 – August 11, 1995) was an American actor, comedian, bandleader, and musician. He was an orchestra leader and a pioneer in radio situation comedy, first with The Jack Benny Program, then in The Phil Harris-Alice Faye Show in which he co-starred with his wife, singer-actress Alice Faye, for eight y…

about_quotes.speaker: Arthur "Monk" Hazel
about_quotes.evidence: He wound up with Phil Harris. And four or five of them. A fella by the name of Charlie DuPont and I kind of helped them out a little bit until they could get some money from California to get back home, you know.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Cobb
about_quotes.evidence: a friend of his was named Phil Grant – I mean, Phil Harris, who was a drummer... We used to hang out with Khalil, Phil, Ben Riley
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Cobb — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Barnet
about_quotes.evidence: I can't think of his name at the moment, but Phil Harris, he didn't even -- he was still the Loughner Lofner Harris Orchestra.

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Sherman
about_quotes.evidence: I said, “Well I wrote radio jingles, ‘tune in tonight and hear Jack Benny and Phil Harris and all that.’”
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Sherman interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, June 1, 2006

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Miller and Pete Fountain
about_quotes.evidence: your old buddy, Phil Harris, was there. Oh, well, he and I do a pretty good job with the Jack Daniels.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Miller and Pete Fountain Interview, 1970-01-09

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: we had Louie Armstrong group, Phil Harris, Florence Henderson, just everybody in show business.
